result:
I’d make it impossible for people to dehumanize each other.

Not force kindness, not erase conflict, not turn everyone into the same person—but remove that mental switch that lets someone see another human as less real, less worthy, or easier to harm. Most of the worst things we do—to individuals or at scale—depend on that gap. If you can’t reduce someone to a label, a stereotype, or an abstraction, cruelty gets a lot harder to justify.

People would still disagree, compete, even fight—but there’d be a floor under how far that could go. You could still say “you’re wrong,” but not “you don’t matter.”

It wouldn’t solve everything. But it would change the direction of almost everything.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
